The platform has experienced explosive growth since launching, with the U.S. market alone expanding from roughly 4,450 shops in mid-2023 to over 475,000 shops by mid-2025—a staggering 5,000% increase in just two years. TikTok Shop generated **$33.2 billion in global GMV** in 2024, more than tripling its 2023 performance of $11 billion. The U.S. emerged as a dominant market, contributing $9 billion in sales just 16 months after the platform launched stateside in September 2023. Thailand stands out as Southeast Asia’s fastest-growing e-commerce market on TikTok, with the platform reaching $5.9 billion in sales and more than 3 million Thai sellers registered. Sales to U.S. small businesses grew by **70% year-over-year**, and they now account for more than a third of monthly TikTok Shop purchases in the country. TikTok Shop reached **200,000 active sellers** in the UK by late 2024—double the number from the previous year.","score":0.6058229,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.netinfluencer.com/tiktok-shop-small-business-sales-jump-66-percent-as-platform-reshapes-product-discovery","title":"TikTok Shop Small Business Sales Jump 66% As Platform Reshapes ...","content":"U.S. small businesses on TikTok Shop increased sales by 66% in 2025 compared to the prior year, the company told Modern Retail. In the UK, more than 200,000 small and midsize businesses have set up storefronts on the platform. The figures accompany a new GlobalData report commissioned by TikTok Shop, based on a nationally representative survey of 6,000 U.S. consumers ages 16 and older conducted in April and May. Patrick Nommensen, Head of Strategic Initiatives at TikTok Shop, said the influx of larger brands onto the platform has not slowed growth for smaller sellers. About 58% of consumers who discovered a small brand on TikTok Shop subsequently purchased from that brand, with more than half completing the purchase within days and roughly one-fifth buying on the same day. The report found that 70% of TikTok Shop consumers have purchased a product recommended by a creator, and 88% of those purchases occurred within the past year.","score":0.5114976,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.modernretail.co/technology/sales-from-major-brands-on-tiktok-shop-nearly-doubled-in-2025-drawing-ulta-and-sally-beauty","title":"Sales from major brands on TikTok Shop nearly doubled in 2025","content":"TikTok Shop logged more than 103 billion U.S. searches with e-commerce intent in 2025, while total transaction volume rose nearly 80% year over","score":0.48411235,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/catherineerdly/2026/02/16/the-tiktok-shop-sellers-behind-social-commerces-87-billion-surge","title":"The TikTok Shop Sellers Behind Social Commerce’s $87 Billion Surge","content":"# The TikTok Shop Sellers Behind Social Commerce’s $87 Billion Surge. Livestreaming and social commerce are the fastest growing sectors of e-commerce, and for some brands, this growth is creating a modern day gold rush. Luke Arnel Cameron didn’t launch his first year on TikTok Shop with a warehouse, a call center, or a giant ad budget. TikTok Shop is increasingly acting like a modern gold rush, creating breakout businesses from kitchens and living rooms, at a speed traditional e-commerce rarely delivers. The growth in live streaming is another facet of the broader phenomenon of “shoppertainment” or shopping as entertainment, which is set to be a major retail trend this year. By the end of 2025, TikTok Shop was an estimated $15.8 billion U.S. ecommerce business, according to eMarketer, putting it close to one-fifth of the country’s social commerce market. And Cameron can testify to this as he’s about to sign a contract for a massive warehouse and retail space.","score":0.4017588,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.44,"request_id":"9036660c-2dce-47b6-9e1a-888541e2ceac"}
